name: voiphopper
title: voiphopper

commands.name: voiphopper

arguments.metavar: list available interfaces for CDP sniffing then exit
arguments.raw: -l (list available interfaces for CDP sniffing, then exit)

arguments.metavar: Spoof the MAC Address then exit
Delete the VLAN Interface then exit
Print the VoIP Hopper version then exit
arguments.raw: -V (Print the VoIP Hopper version, then exit)

arguments.metavar: Spoof the MAC Address of only new Voice Interface
arguments.raw: -D -m (Spoof the MAC Address of only new Voice Interface)

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# voiphopper -h
 VoIP Hopper Extended Usage:
 
 Miscellaneous Options:
 	-l (list available interfaces for CDP sniffing, then exit)
 	Example:  voiphopper -l
 	-m (Spoof the MAC Address, then exit)
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
 	-d (Delete the VLAN Interface, then exit)
 	Example:  voiphopper -d eth0.200
 	-V (Print the VoIP Hopper version, then exit)
 	Example:  voiphopper -V
 
 MAC Address Spoofing Options (used with -a, -v, or -c options):
 	-m (Spoof the MAC Address of existing interface, and new Interface)
 	-D -m (Spoof the MAC Address of only new Voice Interface)
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -D -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
 
 CDP Sniff Mode (-c 0)
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -c 0
 
 CDP Spoof Mode (-c 1):
 	-E <string> (Device ID)
 	-P <string> (Port ID)
 	-C <string> (Capabilities)
 	-L <string> (Platform)
 	-S <string> (Software)
 	-U <string> (Duplex)
 
 Example Usage for SIP Firmware Phone:
 voiphopper -i eth0 -c 1 -E 'SIP00070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P003-08-8-00' -U 1
 
 Example Usage for SCCP Firmware Phone:
 voiphopper -i eth0 -c 1 -E 'SEP0070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P00308000700' -U 1
 
 Example Usage for Phone with MAC Spoofing:
 voiphopper -i eth0 -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86 -c 1 -E 'SEP00070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P003-08-8-00' -U 1
 
 Avaya DHCP Option Mode (-a):
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -a
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -a -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
 
 VLAN Hop Mode (-v VLAN ID):
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -v 200
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -v 200 -D -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
 
 Alcatel VLAN Discovery (-t 0|1|2):
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 0
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 1
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 0 -m 00:80:9f:ad:42:42
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 1 -m 00:80:9f:ad:42:42
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 2 -v 800
 	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 2 -v 800 -m 00:80:9f:ad:42:42

examples.command:
voiphopper -i eth0 -z
VoIP Hopper assessment mode ~ Select 'q' to quit and 'h' for help menu.
Main Sniffer:  capturing packets on eth0
a
Analyzing ARP packets on default interface: eth0
New host #1 learned on eth0: (MAC): 78:ca:39:fe:0b:4c   (IP): 192.168.1.229
New host #2 learned on eth0: (MAC): 60:6b:bd:5a:b6:6c   (IP): 192.168.1.213
New host #3 learned on eth0: (MAC): 40:6c:8f:1b:cb:90   (IP): 192.168.1.232

local_help.--help:
VoIP Hopper Extended Usage:

Miscellaneous Options:
	-l (list available interfaces for CDP sniffing, then exit)
	Example:  voiphopper -l
	-m (Spoof the MAC Address, then exit)
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
	-d (Delete the VLAN Interface, then exit)
	Example:  voiphopper -d eth0.200
	-V (Print the VoIP Hopper version, then exit)
	Example:  voiphopper -V

MAC Address Spoofing Options (used with -a, -v, or -c options):
	-m (Spoof the MAC Address of existing interface, and new Interface)
	-D -m (Spoof the MAC Address of only new Voice Interface)
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -D -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86

CDP Sniff Mode (-c 0)
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -c 0

CDP Spoof Mode (-c 1):
	-E <string> (Device ID)
	-P <string> (Port ID)
	-C <string> (Capabilities)
	-L <string> (Platform)
	-S <string> (Software)
	-U <string> (Duplex)

Example Usage for SIP Firmware Phone:
voiphopper -i eth0 -c 1 -E 'SIP00070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P003-08-8-00' -U 1

Example Usage for SCCP Firmware Phone:
voiphopper -i eth0 -c 1 -E 'SEP0070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P00308000700' -U 1

Example Usage for Phone with MAC Spoofing:
voiphopper -i eth0 -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86 -c 1 -E 'SEP00070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P003-08-8-00' -U 1

Avaya DHCP Option Mode (-a):
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -a
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -a -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86

VLAN Hop Mode (-v VLAN ID):
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -v 200
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -v 200 -D -m 00:07:0E:EA:50:86

Alcatel VLAN Discovery (-t 0|1|2):
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 0
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 1
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 0 -m 00:80:9f:ad:42:42
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 1 -m 00:80:9f:ad:42:42
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 2 -v 800
	Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 2 -v 800 -m 00:80:9f:ad:42:42

local_help.help:
voiphopper -i <interface> -c {0|1|2} -l <DEVICEID> -a -n -v <VLANID>

Please specify 1 base option mode:

LLDP Spoof Mode (-o 001EF7289C8E)
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -o 001EF7289C8E

CDP Sniff Mode (-c 0)
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -c 0

CDP Spoof Mode with custom packet (-c 1):
-E <string> (Device ID)
-P <string> (Port ID)
-C <string> (Capabilities)
-L <string> (Platform)
-S <string> (Software)
-U <string> (Duplex)
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -c 1 -E 'SIP00070EEA5086' -P 'Port 1' -C Host -L 'Cisco IP Phone 7940' -S 'P003-08-8-00' -U 1

CDP Spoof Mode with pre-made packet (-c 2)
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -c 2

Avaya DHCP Option Mode (-a):
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -a

VLAN Hop Mode (-v VLAN ID):
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -v 200

Nortel DHCP Option Mode (-n):
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -n

Alcatel Mode (-t 0|1):
Example:  voiphopper -i eth0 -t 0

'voiphopper -h' for more help

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=3b2b8fed966f446d0381e063dcaec0565bcdc4a6, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Runs a VLAN hop security test
VoIP Hopper is a GPLv3 licensed security tool, written in C
that rapidly runs a VLAN Hop security test.  VoIP Hopper is
a VoIP infrastructure security testing tool but also a tool
that can be used to test the (in)security of VLANs.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use voiphopper because ### voiphopper.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair voiphopper with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `voiphopper --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### voiphopper.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install voiphopper
